# QB Ablation Experiment

*Generated by `sportslab qb-ablation` (v2.0.0, 2026-06-23)*

## Overview

Three model variants compared across rolling simulation (all eligible seasons week-by-week), fitted-once (holdout), bootstrap confidence intervals, and QB-change subset.

## Models

| Model | Features | QB Source |
|-------|----------|-----------|
| Incumbent | qb_changed + rolling_mov_3 | Oracle (actual starter) |
| No-QB | rolling_mov_3 only | None |
| Live-QB | qb_changed + rolling_mov_3 | CSV pregame input |

## Rolling Simulation (All Seasons)

| Model | Log Loss | Brier | Accuracy | Games |
|-------|----------|-------|----------|-------|
| incumbent | 0.6487 | 0.2282 | 0.6329 | 1373 |
| no_qb | 0.651 | 0.2293 | 0.6256 | 1373 |

### Delta vs Incumbent (Rolling)

No-QB Δ LL: **0.0021** [95% CI: -0.0053, 0.0096]

## Fitted-Once (Holdout: 2025)

| Model | Log Loss | Brier | Accuracy | Games |
|-------|----------|-------|----------|-------|
| incumbent | 0.6262 | 0.218 | 0.663 | 276 |
| no_qb | 0.6255 | 0.2176 | 0.6667 | 276 |

## QB-Change Subset Analysis

| Model | Log Loss | Games |
|-------|----------|-------|
| incumbent | 0.6363 | 282 |
| no_qb | 0.6492 | 282 |

## Recommendation

Rolling Δ (no-QB − incumbent): +0.0023
Fitted-once Δ (no-QB − incumbent): -0.0007

**Mixed results — no promotion.**

Incumbent wins rolling (0.6487 vs 0.6510), no-QB wins fitted-once (0.6262 vs 0.6255). Neither beats on both. The qb_changed signal is real (QB-change subset: 0.6363 vs 0.6492) but too weak to dominate on aggregate. The bootstrap CI includes zero, confirming the difference is not statistically significant.

**Recommendation:** Continue with incumbent as research benchmark. If live-pregame deployment requires avoiding oracle QB dependencies, the no-QB variant is a safe fallback with near-identical aggregate performance.

---
*Incumbent: v2.0.0, 0.6262 holdout LL.*
